title: JIT code cache and generation-based invalidation
purpose: Compiled native code must be stored and invalidated when modules are hot-reloaded. The JIT cache maps (module, function, arity, generation) to compiled NativeCode. Hot code loading (B-041–B-045) uses a two-generation scheme (current/old). purge_module removes the old generation; force_purge_module removes both. The cache must respect these semantics. Per D6 in the JIT/AOT design doc: in-flight calls to old code complete normally; new calls use the current generation.
task: Implement JitCache in crates/beamr/src/jit/cache.rs. The cache stores Arc<NativeCode> values keyed by (Atom, Atom, u8, u32) representing (module, function, arity, generation). Provide lookup, insertion, per-module eviction (by generation), and force-eviction (both generations). The cache is thread-safe — concurrent lookups from scheduler threads must not block. This brief delivers the cache data structure and its invalidation API only. Wiring the cache into the interpreter dispatch loop is B-135.

requirements.title: JitCache data structure
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a JitCache struct backed by DashMap<CacheKey, Arc<NativeCode>> where CacheKey is (Atom, Atom, u8, u32) representing (module, function, arity, generation). THE SYSTEM SHALL implement Send + Sync for JitCache. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use a Mutex or RwLock for the top-level cache — DashMap provides concurrent shard-level locking.
acceptance: JitCache::new() creates an empty cache
CacheKey is a tuple struct CacheKey(Atom, Atom, u8, u32) implementing Hash + Eq
JitCache is Send + Sync (static assertion via fn assert_send_sync<T: Send + Sync>() {})
Internal storage is DashMap<CacheKey, Arc<NativeCode>>
Test: JitCache::new() followed by len() returns 0

requirements.title: Cache insertion
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide JitCache::insert(module, function, arity, generation, code) that stores an Arc<NativeCode> under the given key. WHEN a key already exists, THE SYSTEM SHALL replace the old entry. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT block other cache operations during insertion beyond the DashMap shard lock.
acceptance: JitCache::insert(module: Atom, function: Atom, arity: u8, generation: u32, code: Arc<NativeCode>) stores the entry
Duplicate insert overwrites the previous entry
JitCache::len() increments after insert of a new key
Test: insert two entries with different keys, len() == 2
Test: insert same key twice, len() == 1, lookup returns the second value

requirements.title: Cache lookup
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide JitCache::lookup(module, function, arity, generation) that returns Option<Arc<NativeCode>>. WHEN the key exists, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Some(code) with an Arc clone (no move). WHEN the key does not exist, THE SYSTEM SHALL return None. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT return compiled code for a different generation than requested.
acceptance: JitCache::lookup(module: Atom, function: Atom, arity: u8, generation: u32) -> Option<Arc<NativeCode>>
Lookup for an existing key returns Some with the correct NativeCode
Lookup for a non-existent key returns None
Lookup for existing (module, function, arity) but wrong generation returns None
Test: insert at generation 1, lookup generation 1 -> Some, lookup generation 2 -> None
Test: lookup on empty cache returns None

requirements.title: Generation-based eviction on purge_module
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide JitCache::purge_generation(module, generation) that removes all entries matching the given module and generation. WHEN entries for other generations of the same module exist, THE SYSTEM SHALL leave them intact. THE SYSTEM SHALL minimise blocking via DashMap shard-level locking during eviction. In-flight callers holding an Arc<NativeCode> from before the eviction SHALL continue to execute — the Arc reference keeps the code alive until dropped.
acceptance: JitCache::purge_generation(module: Atom, generation: u32) removes all entries for that module+generation
Entries for the same module at a different generation are untouched
Entries for other modules are untouched
After purge, lookup for the evicted generation returns None
Arc<NativeCode> cloned before purge remains valid after purge (Arc::strong_count >= 1)
Test: insert foo/bar/1 at gen 0 and gen 1, purge gen 0, lookup gen 0 -> None, lookup gen 1 -> Some
Test: insert foo/bar/1 and baz/qux/2 at gen 0, purge foo gen 0, baz/qux/2 still present

requirements.title: Force-purge eviction for both generations
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide JitCache::force_purge_module(module) that removes all entries for the given module across all generations. This corresponds to force_purge_module in the hot code loading system. THE SYSTEM SHALL use DashMap::retain for in-place eviction, avoiding allocation of a separate key collection.
acceptance: JitCache::force_purge_module(module: Atom) removes all entries where key.0 == module
Entries for other modules are untouched
Test: insert foo/bar/1 at gen 0 and gen 1, force_purge foo, both lookups return None
Test: force_purge on a module with no cached entries is a no-op (no panic)

requirements.title: Cache statistics for profiler integration
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide JitCache::len() returning the number of cached entries, JitCache::contains(module, function, arity, generation) returning bool, and JitCache::cached_functions_for_module(module) returning a Vec of (function, arity, generation) tuples for the given module. These support the profiler (B-130 R4) in avoiding re-compilation of already-cached functions.
acceptance: JitCache::len() -> usize returns the total number of cached entries
JitCache::contains(module, function, arity, generation) -> bool returns true if the key exists
JitCache::cached_functions_for_module(module) -> Vec<(Atom, u8, u32)> lists all cached entries for that module
Test: insert 3 entries for module foo, 1 for module bar, cached_functions_for_module(foo) returns 3 entries
Test: contains returns false after purge_generation removes the entry

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement cache size limits or eviction policies — the cache is unbounded
SHALL NOT implement speculative deoptimisation — simple eviction only
SHALL NOT wire the cache into the interpreter dispatch loop — dispatch integration is B-135
SHALL NOT implement inline caching or polymorphic dispatch caches
SHALL NOT deallocate native code synchronously during eviction — Arc reference counting handles deallocation when the last reference drops
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
cache.rs is under 500 lines of production code
JitCache is Send + Sync (compile-time assertion in tests)
All cache operations use DashMap — no Mutex or RwLock on the top-level structure
